# The Challenge | Quick Reference (condensed day-by-day)

> The condensed map. For the full guide (lessons, key teaching points, trainer video briefs, and checkpoints), see **The Campaign | 15-Day Challenge Host Guide**.
> All dates and facts come from `01 - FACTS`. All times **SGT**.

---

## Your role in one line

Each day you deliver a short lesson (record or live) that **teaches one piece of the agent and sets one ~30-minute task.** By Day 15 every member has a working AI marketing agent that, every morning, hands them a fresh content idea + a ready-to-run prompt. The host (Matt) replies to submissions daily and runs the gates.

- **Tool:** Claude (Projects). No design tools, no code, no images.
- **Each lesson:** ~10 to 15 min teaching → a ~30-min student task → one thing to post.
- **Voice:** realistic, plain, encouraging. *"Your first agent,"* never *"automate your whole business."* No jargon.
- **Templates:** every task has a paste-ready template in `14 - Templates Bundle`. Tell students: paste, fill the [brackets], run.

---

## The destination (what you're building them toward)

> Show this **finished agent in action on Day 1** so they see where they're headed: a member types "today's idea," and the agent replies with a fresh, on-trend content idea for *their* business + a ready-to-run prompt to make it. That's the whole promise, no more blank page.

---

## The 15 days: what you teach, what they do, what they submit

Legend: 🟠 graded submission · 🔴 gate (miss it = out of the competition)

### Day 1: Sun Jun 21 · Orientation

- **Teach:** Welcome. What the challenge is, what they'll have by Jul 5, how it works (30 min/day, 2 gates). **Demo the finished agent.**
- **Student task:** Join the community, create a Claude Project named "My Marketing Agent," post a hello (what they sell).
- **Submit:** hello in `#Day1` (not graded).
- **Say:** *"By Jul 5 you'll have an agent that hands you an idea every morning. 30 minutes a day. Two deadlines you can't miss, Day 8 and Day 14. It's free with your membership."*

### Day 2: Mon Jun 22 · Name your business

- **Teach:** Why the agent needs to know your business; how to write a tight 3-sentence description.
- **Task:** Name business + product in 3 sentences inside the Project.
- **Submit:** `#Day2`.
- **Say:** *"Garbage in, garbage out, the clearer your business, the better the ideas."*

### Day 3: Tue Jun 23 · Build the business profile 🟠

- **Teach:** The full business profile, product, price, the one differentiator, the outcome the customer buys, channels (Template 2).
- **Task:** Write the profile into the Project.
- **Submit:** `#Day3`, **graded.**
- **Say:** *"Name the ONE thing competitors don't have. Specific beats generic every time."*

### Day 4: Wed Jun 24 · Teach it your customer

- **Teach:** Who buys, their main problem, where they hang out, the words they use.
- **Task:** Add the ideal customer (Template 3).
- **Submit:** `#Day4`.
- **Say:** *"The sharper the customer's pain, the sharper the ideas."*

### Day 5: Thu Jun 25 · Teach it your voice

- **Teach:** How to make the agent sound like them, paste 2 to 3 writing samples (Template 4).
- **Task:** Add voice samples.
- **Submit:** `#Day5`.
- **Say:** *"Make it sound like you, not a robot. Ask it to write one line and check the vibe."*

### Day 6: Fri Jun 26 · Build the idea engine 🟠 *(the heart: spend the most time here)*

- **Teach:** The core instruction that makes the agent research and produce ONE daily content idea (Template 5). Walk through it slowly.
- **Task:** Build the idea engine, run it once, post the idea it gave them.
- **Submit:** `#Day6`, **graded (first agent draft).**
- **Say:** *"This is the engine. Everything after today just makes it better."*

### Day 7: Sat Jun 27 · First ideas

- **Teach:** Generating and judging ideas, run it 3×, keep the best.
- **Task:** Generate 3 ideas, keep the best one.
- **Submit:** `#Day7`.
- **Say:** *"Trust your gut on which one you'd actually post."*

### Day 8: Sun Jun 28 · 🔴 GATE 1: the 2-minute video

- **Teach:** Why this gate exists (it's the un-quitting move) and how to film, phone, no editing, no script.
- **Task:** A **2-minute phone video**: show the agent produce one idea + say their biggest blocker. Post by **11:59pm**.
- **Submit:** `#Day8`, **🔴 GATE. Miss it = out of the competition.**
- **Say:** *"2 minutes, phone, no editing. Hit record, say one idea + one blocker, post. This is the only video in the whole challenge, miss it and you're out of the running."*

### Day 9: Mon Jun 29 · Idea → ready-to-run prompt

- **Teach:** Turn each idea into a paste-ready prompt (post / reel / caption) (Template 6).
- **Task:** Add the prompt step.
- **Submit:** `#Day9`.
- **Say:** *"Now it hands you something you can paste. That's the moment it goes from toy to tool."*

### Day 10: Tue Jun 30 · Three formats 🟠

- **Teach:** One idea → a LinkedIn/FB post, a reel script, a caption (Template 7).
- **Task:** Make the agent output 3 formats.
- **Submit:** `#Day10`, **graded.**

### Day 11: Wed Jul 1 · Make it proactive

- **Teach:** Set the daily routine so it greets them with an idea (Template 8).
- **Task:** Set up the routine.
- **Submit:** `#Day11`.
- **Say:** *"Now it greets you with an idea, no more blank page."*

### Day 12: Thu Jul 2 · Tighten it 🟠

- **Teach:** Kill anything generic; make every idea name their product or customer.
- **Task:** Polish the instructions.
- **Submit:** `#Day12`, **graded.**

### Day 13: Fri Jul 3 · Prove it for a week

- **Teach:** Run it 7 times in one sitting, a full week of ideas.
- **Task:** Generate a week of ideas, pick the favorite.
- **Submit:** `#Day13`.

### Day 14: Sat Jul 4 · 🔴 GATE 2: the final submission

- **Teach:** **Exactly** what to submit and how, three things, in **three separate boxes** on the form: (1) the agent (a link or a 60 to 90 sec screen recording), (2) the one-page brief (Template 9), (3) one demo idea + prompt. Each box takes a link OR a file.
- **Task:** Submit the package in `#Day14` by **11:59pm**, this is their competition entry.
- **Submit:** `#Day14`, **🔴 GATE = competition entry.**
- **Say:** *"Three boxes, agent, brief, demo. A link or a file, either works. Don't polish, a working agent beats a perfect plan you never send."*

### Day 15: Sun Jul 5 · 🎓 Graduation

- **Teach:** Recap and celebrate. Prep for the Summit, bring your agent, **be present to win.**
- **Task:** Post the best idea the agent gave them.
- **Submit:** `#Day15`.
- **Say:** *"You built a working AI marketing agent. Most people never start. Summit is Wednesday, winners are announced live, so be there."*

---

## The two gates (drill these into every session)

| Gate | Day | What | Deadline |
|---|---|---|---|
| 🔴 Gate 1 | Day 8 (Sun Jun 28) | 2-minute phone video | 11:59pm SGT |
| 🔴 Gate 2 | Day 14 (Sat Jul 4) | Final agent + brief + demo (3 boxes) | 11:59pm SGT |

**Miss either → off the graduation/competition track** (they can still attend the Summit). Remind them in the days *before* each gate, not just on the day.

---

## The Summit: Wed Jul 8 · 11:00pm to 2:00am SGT (3 hours, live)

- **3 expert trainers each build a different agent live**, so members see what's possible beyond their first build:
  - **Research Agent**, finds trends + competitor moves for your business
  - **Content/Image Agent**, turns an idea into ready-to-post content
  - **Outreach/Lead Agent**, finds + sorts people ready to buy
- Top graduates are featured. **Winners ($1,000 in credits: $500 / $300 / $100 / $100, the 4th is a community-voted Community MVP) are announced live, members must be present.**
- Full run-of-show, timings, and roles: see `11 - Summit Run-Sheet`.

---

## Teaching guidelines (every session)

1. End every lesson with the three things on screen: **what to do · how long · what to post.**
2. Realistic outcomes only, "your first agent," never "10x your revenue."
3. Plain language. If a 1st-timer wouldn't understand it, cut it.
4. Point them to the template for the day (`14 - Templates Bundle`), paste, fill brackets, run.
5. Protect Day 6 (the idea engine), it's the heart; go slow.

## What to emphasize to drive completion

- **The destination**, the agent that feeds them an idea every morning.
- **The two gates** (Day 8, Day 14), every session before them.
- **"30 minutes a day"**, it's achievable.
- The prize + the live winner reveal at the Summit.

---

## Trainer FAQ (the questions you'll hit)

- **Are the daily lessons live or pre-recorded?** Pre-recorded short videos posted each day; the host (Matt) replies to submissions live. *(Confirm with ops if any day runs live.)*
- **Do I grade submissions?** No, the host replies/grades daily using `12 - Feedback Templates`. You teach and set the task.
- **How long should each lesson be?** ~10 to 15 min teaching → a ~30-min student task. Always end with: *what to do · how long · what to post.*
- **What Claude plan do students need?** A Claude account with **Projects**. [ team to confirm: free plan, or is a paid plan required? ]
- **A student's ideas feel generic, what do I tell them?** Their business profile (Day 3) and customer (Day 4) aren't detailed enough. Send them back to those. Day 12 is a polish day.
- **Someone missed a gate, are they out?** Off the graduation/competition track, but they can still attend the Summit and keep their agent. Offer a 1:1 if they're close.
- **What if I can't demo a step live?** Use the exact prompts in `14 - Templates Bundle`, paste and run.
- **Where do people drop off?** Around the middle. Protect **Day 6** (the idea engine, the heart) and hammer the **Day 8 gate**.
- **What do students need?** Just Claude + their phone (for the Day 8 video). No design tools, no code.
- **Where do students post?** The `#Day1 … #Day15` channels.
- **What's submitted and when is it graded?** 6 graded days, **Days 3, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14**; Day 8 and Day 14 are the gates (see the day-by-day above).
